name: KiCad Routing Tools
description: Fast Rust-accelerated A* autorouter for KiCad PCBs.
description_full:
KiCad Routing Tools is a fast, grid-based A* autorouter for KiCad PCB files, accelerated with a native Rust core (~10x faster than pure Python).

Features:
- Octilinear routing on multiple copper layers with automatic via insertion
- Differential pair routing with pose-based A* and orientation-aware setbacks
- Rip-up and reroute with blocking analysis when routes fail
- BGA / QFN fanout, GND-via placement for return paths, polarity swap detection
- Net ordering strategies (MPS, inside-out, original) with crossing-conflict layer swaps
- Board-edge and BGA exclusion-zone clearance, plus stub / BGA / track proximity penalties
- Plane routing and disconnected-plane stitching
- Schematic synchronization when target pads are swapped

Integrates with the KiCad PCB Editor as an Action Plugin (Tools > External Plugins). Also usable from the command line for scripting and CI.

The package bundles prebuilt Rust binaries for Linux x86_64, macOS arm64 (Apple Silicon) + x86_64 (Intel), and Windows x86_64; a small startup resolver picks the right binary for the running platform.

First launch will offer to install scipy and shapely into KiCad's Python (KiCad bundles numpy + wxPython but not these two).
